title: Viguiera cornifolia
taxonomy: Viguiera cornifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is also known as the horned cudweed. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Viguiera cornifolia is classified within the larger group of daisy-like plants. The plant's classification can be further broken down into the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida.

intro: Viguiera cornifolia, also known as Tree Flower or Tree Daisy, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions like wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Viguiera cornifolia include flavonoids, terpenoids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often employed to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
